meta.title: VibeTerm — a modern terminal manager built for vibe coding
meta.description: A local terminal for keeping tabs on a bunch of AI agents at once: which one's working, which one's stuck, which one's pinging you. CJK done right, no login, nothing leaves your machine, offline by default. Tauri + Rust + SolidJS, MIT.
meta.ogAlt: VibeTerm — a modern terminal that gets CJK right
nav.theme.label: Switch theme
nav.lang.label: Switch language
hero.title:
Sparks everywhere,
clear at a glance.
hero.subtitle: A modern terminal manager built for vibe coding. Local-first, CJK-native. Let the agents run — who's burning, who's burned out, who's pinging you — all in plain sight. No intrusion, no login, no cloud.
hero.cta.note: macOS · Windows · always free and open source
hero.win.title: vibeterm
section.principles.title: It doesn't run your agents for you. It just keeps an eye on them.
principle.zeroIntrusion.title: Never touches your config
principle.zeroIntrusion.desc: It works out state by sniffing: reading output and read-only file watching. It never writes to ~/.claude or ~/.codex, installs no hooks, runs no background services. Not a byte of your agent config gets touched.
principle.multiAgent.title: Keeps a bunch of agents in check
principle.multiAgent.desc: Agents get messy once you've got a few. It floats the stuck ones and the ones waiting on you to the top, so you're not clicking through each one to see who needs you.
principle.terminal.title: A terminal stays a terminal
principle.terminal.desc: It does the terminal basics well. No feature bloat, no ambition to become an agent workbench.
principle.cjk.title: CJK that just works
principle.cjk.desc: Wide characters, IME input, copying text with emoji in it — the things English terminals keep getting wrong. Handled here.
principle.privacy.title: Everything stays on your machine
principle.privacy.desc: No login, no data collection, offline by default. It only goes online when you check for updates — and even then, it only reads.
principle.oss.title: MIT, open source
principle.oss.desc: All the code is public. Read it, change it, do whatever you want with it.
section.features.title: Everything a terminal should do — plus the agent layer.
section.features.subtitle: All the usual terminal features, plus state-awareness and orchestration built for a screen full of AI agents.
features.sniff.label: Sees what an agent's doing
features.sniff.desc: Working, waiting, stalled, or done — figured out without touching your config.
features.urgency.label: Stall detection + urgency sort
features.urgency.desc: Screen full of agents? The stuck ones and the ones waiting on you go to the top.
features.monitor.label: Live usage
features.monitor.desc: Context left, 5h/7d quota, burn rate, cache, cost — all on one bar.
features.stats.label: Usage stats
features.stats.desc: Token and cost numbers for Claude / Codex. Computed offline, exportable.
features.split.label: Splits + worktrees
features.split.desc: Mount a git worktree, one terminal tree per task.
features.canvas.label: Canvas board
features.canvas.desc: Lay tasks out as cards, drag-select, send one command to several terminals.
features.floating.label: Floating windows
features.floating.desc: Pop any task into its own window and keep watching.
features.render.label: GPU rendering
features.render.desc: WebGL-accelerated — and CJK still won't drop glyphs or stutter.
features.palette.label: Command palette
features.palette.desc: Custom keybindings and actions — drive the whole thing from the keyboard.
features.prompts.label: Prompt presets
features.prompts.desc: Handy presets for claude / codex / shell, a keystroke away.
features.statusbar.label: Configurable status bar
features.statusbar.desc: Drag the widgets around; each agent type gets its own layout.
features.notify.label: Desktop notifications
features.notify.desc: 24 built-in sounds + quiet hours, only when an agent's state changes.
features.theme.label: Hot-swap themes
features.theme.desc: 10 built-in themes, switch anytime, macOS and Windows.
section.mechanism.title: How does it know what an agent's doing without touching your config?
section.mechanism.subtitle: Three layers of sniffing, plus read-only file watching. No hooks, no login, nothing written.
mechanism.layer1.title: OSC 133 / 633 sequences
mechanism.layer1.desc: Command boundary markers from shell integration. The most reliable layer: it knows exactly when a command starts, ends, or sits waiting for input.
mechanism.layer2.title: Reading agent output
mechanism.layer2.desc: Matches the approval prompts of 11 common agents to tell when one's waiting on you.
mechanism.layer3.title: That spinner in the title bar
mechanism.layer3.desc: If the braille spinner in the window title is moving, the agent's working.
mechanism.redline.title: The one rule: hands off your stuff
mechanism.redline.desc: Never writes ~/.claude or ~/.codex, installs no hooks, runs no background services. Every state is watched, never injected.
mechanism.states.title: Five states, clear at a glance.
states.running.label: Running
states.running.desc: Steady blue dot with a glow. The agent's working.
states.waiting.label: Waiting
states.waiting.desc: Amber dot, breathing — it's waiting on you. Worth a look.
states.stalled.label: Stalled
states.stalled.desc: Red-orange ring. Quiet for over 5 minutes, probably stuck.
states.idle.label: Idle
states.idle.desc: Still grey dot. Nothing going on.
states.done.label: Done
states.done.desc: Outlined ring, struck through. This one's actually finished.
section.cjk.title: Not one of the major English AI terminals takes CJK seriously.
section.cjk.subtitle: Almost every major AI-terminal repo has CJK bugs sitting open, buried under English users' urgent ones. Nobody's really done this part. VibeTerm treats it as actual work.
cjk.vibeterm.title: On VibeTerm's side
cjk.point.ime: IME composition held the whole way (isComposing / keyCode 229). No misfires, no lag.
cjk.point.width: Wide and ambiguous widths measured right, so tables stay lined up.
cjk.point.wrap: Chinese line-wrap doesn't truncate; streaming never splits a glyph.
cjk.point.copy: Copy is guarded by Intl.Segmenter, so it won't tear surrogate pairs or break ZWJ emoji.
cjk.point.render: CJK doesn't drop or shift under GPU rendering.
cjk.point.notify: Native notifications when a background agent does something.
cjk.conclusion: Data is current as of publication. Spot something off? PRs welcome.
section.themes.title: Switch the theme, the whole site follows.
section.themes.subtitle: The product's 10 built-in terminal themes are right here on this site. Click one and everything follows, from the nav to the terminal window.
section.credits.title: Standing on these shoulders.
section.credits.subtitle: VibeTerm uses and borrows from these open-source projects.
credits.thanks: Special thanks to ryoppippi's ccusage (MIT). The usage stats, model pricing, and 5-hour blocks all drew from it; pricing data comes from LiteLLM and Anthropic's official numbers.
section.download.title: Give it a go?
section.download.subtitle: Free, open source, everything local. Download and run, no signup.
download.note: macOS 11+ and Windows — same download page.
footer.tagline: Sparks everywhere, clear at a glance.
